## CONTEXT ROADMAP
(Index of what the context contains and where to find it)

[cr-00062] Benson paper (ALE/FSI): §1.6.2 Multidimensional transport by operator splitting – advection algorithm requirements (conservation, monotonicity, second-order accuracy), monotonicity condition, consistency condition (φ(1)=1), TVD limiter functions (minmod, superbee, van Leer). Approx char offsets 75500-87000; consistency condition paragraph near 83700-84600.

[cr-00065] Corpus includes paper '水下爆炸冲击荷载作用下混凝土重力坝的破坏模式' (Zhang et al., 2012) detailing HJC concrete model parameters for underwater explosion damage simulation, including tensile failure strain threshold.

[cr-00071] Benson paper §1.4.7 – Mixture theories (multi-material elements): covers mean strain rate mixture theory, mean stress mixture theory, and strategies for extreme stiffness ratios (e.g., steel+vacuum). Approx char offsets: 78800-82000.

[cr-00074] Benson paper §1.6.5.2 Level set methods: implicit interface representation via signed distance function, advection, reinitialization, comparison with VOF. Approx char offsets 112000-117000. Also §5.2.1 (Chapter 5) 'The level set or signed distance functions' near char offset 9750.

## CONTEXT UNDERSTANDING
(High-level understanding of the context: what it is, how it's organized, and what matters)

[cu-00063] Corpus is a mix of machine-learning research papers and engineering/mechanics papers (explicit FE codes, ALE, FSI, concrete dam failure). Key papers: Benson (ALE/FSI/hourglass) and Zhang et al. 2012 (HJC concrete model for underwater explosion).

## DOMAIN CONSTANTS
(Exact parameters, formulas, thresholds, reference values, enum sets, and output field requirements defined by the context.)

[dc-00066] HJC concrete model parameters (Zhang et al., 2012): ρ=2.4 t/m³, G=14.86 GPa, A=0.79, B=1.60, C=0.007, N=0.61, f′c=48 MPa, D1=0.04, D2=1.0, tensile failure strain threshold=0.002.

[dc-00070] Central difference method (Benson paper): second-order accurate; truncation error proportional to (Δt)³.

[dc-00073] Advection consistency condition: limiter function φ(r) where r=Δφ_{i-1/2}/Δφ_{i+1/2} must satisfy φ(1)=1. When r=1 (smooth data) ensures second-order accuracy (Lax-Wendroff or Fromm). Works with monotonicity condition to define admissible TVD region.

## PARSING SCHEMA
(How to parse and navigate the context's format)

## REUSABLE RESULTS
(Reusable knowledge about the context)

[rr-00068] Benson paper advection requirements: (1) Conservation – total quantity preserved; (2) Monotonicity – no new extrema, existing not amplified; (3) Second-order accuracy – to limit diffusion. Consistency condition (φ(1)=1) with monotonicity defines admissible TVD region for limiter functions, balancing accuracy and stability.

[rr-00069] Operator splitting in ALE: decomposes PDE into (1) Lagrangian step (w=0, solve source terms); (2) Eulerian step (advection -w ∂ϕ/∂x). Approximates e^{(A+B)Δt} by e^{BΔt}e^{AΔt} (first-order), symmetric splitting for second-order. Physically: update on moving mesh, then remap to fixed mesh.

[rr-00072] Mixture theories (Benson §1.4.7): For extreme stiffness ratios (e.g., steel+vacuum), prefer preferentially compressing/expanding soft material first (strategy 2) – avoids over-compression without instability of mean stress theory.

## ERROR PATTERNS
